itemDefinition.title: Adaptive Front-lighting System (AFS)
itemDefinition.boundary: AFS ECU including CAN interface, actuator control logic, and OTA update module
itemDefinition.environment: On-vehicle, exposed to external network via telematics gateway

damageScenarios.title: Malicious firmware update causes headlight failure
damageScenarios.description: An attacker injects a malicious firmware update causing AFS ECU to fail, resulting in loss of forward lighting.

damageScenarios.title: CAN bus spoofing causes incorrect beam adjustment
damageScenarios.description: An attacker spoofs CAN messages causing the headlight beam to point in dangerous directions.

threatScenarios.title: Remote firmware injection via OTA
threatScenarios.cause: Lack of firmware signature verification on OTA update path

threatScenarios.title: CAN bus message spoofing via OBD-II port
threatScenarios.cause: Unauthenticated CAN messages accepted by AFS ECU

attackSteps.title: Attacker signs malicious firmware with stolen certificate key
Vehicle downloads and installs malicious firmware via OTA
Attacker gains physical access to vehicle interior
Attacker replays or crafts malicious CAN frames targeting AFS ECU

attackPaths.title: OTA server compromise leading to firmware injection
Physical OBD-II access for CAN injection

riskThresholdMatrix.rationale: Very High risks pose unacceptable safety or business exposure; only reduction or avoidance is permitted. Escalation to CISO is mandatory.
High risks require active risk reduction or elimination. Risk acceptance or transfer is not permitted without executive sign-off.
Medium risks may be reduced, avoided, or transferred to a third party with appropriate contractual controls. Acceptance requires documented justification.
Low risks may be treated with any strategy. Acceptance is permitted with documented rationale reviewed annually.

attackFeasibilityRatings.rationale: OTA compromise requires significant expertise but is feasible for a motivated nation-state or organized criminal group.
Physical OBD-II access is straightforward for a mechanic-level attacker with basic CAN tools.

impactRatings.rationale: Loss of headlights at night poses immediate safety risk to driver and pedestrians.
Incorrect beam direction poses hazard to oncoming traffic.

riskValues.rationale: High feasibility combined with critical safety impact yields Very High risk.
Medium feasibility with serious but non-critical impact yields Medium risk.

riskTreatmentDecisions.justification: Very High risk band per OEM Risk Acceptance Policy v2 only allows 'reduce' or 'avoid'. Reduce via secure OTA signature verification (ctrl-001).
Medium risk band; chose to reduce via CAN message authentication rather than accept or share.
